fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 76 is inverted: `if not clamp` should be `if clamp`. This causes clamping to be applied when clamp=False and skipped when clamp=True.

spec.expr: (clamp and (y__3 == min(max(y__1, min(y0, y1)), max(y0, y1)))) or (not clamp and y__3 == y__1)

constraints.intent: After line 76, if clamp is True the value should be clamped, otherwise it should be the raw interpolated value.

spec.expr: not clamp and (y__2 >= min(y0, y1)) and (y__2 <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: Inside the then-branch (not clamp), the clamped value should be within [min(y0,y1), max(y0,y1)] only when clamp is actually False.
y__2 (the clamped value) should only be computed when clamp is False, but the inverted condition means it is computed when clamp is True.

spec.expr: (clamp and (y__3 >= min(y0, y1)) and (y__3 <= max(y0, y1))) or (not clamp and y__3 == y__1)

constraints.intent: Before y__3 is used, it should be clamped when clamp=True and unclamped when clamp=False.
